name: Ibaraki Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the sea, the sky, and a hopeful future
the bara (rose) blossom, prefectural flower, and the purity of Ibaraki

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white spiral blossom stylising a rose (bara), the prefectural flower of Ibaraki.
history: Ibaraki's prefectural symbol, designed by Goto Yasuhide, was adopted on 24 November 1991, replacing the earlier 1966 emblem. The flag bearing it on a blue field is in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the blue of the Pacific Ocean off the Ibaraki coast and the open skies of the Kanto plain

features.role: stylised rose (bara) blossom symbolising the prefectural flower
features.symbolism: the rose, prefectural flower of Ibaraki, drawn as a swirling spiral evoking development, the harmony of the prefecture's people, and the wave of the Pacific.
